texto: Where has Arnold been?’ I said. ‘... the Westwoods get in the papers.’‘Patting the orphans.’‘Getting homes for the homeless.’‘Legs for the legless.’ She was laughing as she said it.‘Blind dogs,’ I said. ‘Dogs for the blind, that is.’‘Patting blind dogs.’ We both laughed and drank. ‘Getting legs for them.’‘Homes for legless, blind orphan dogs.’ Maybe we were both a little drunk, on bourbon or on the past.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de James Ferron Anderson que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Where has Arnold been?’ I said. ‘... the Westwoods get in the papers.’‘Patting the orphans.’‘Gett…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, philosophy-of-life, blind, com foco em reflexao.

texto: A well-adjusted person wouldn't talk much. There's not a lot to be said about most of life. Most days, weeks, years, lives, nothing happens... and still we carry on chattering at each other.
explicacao: Citação de James Ferron Anderson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “A well-adjusted person wouldn't talk much. There's not a lot to be said about most of life. Most …” articula reflexao, philosophical, adjusted em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
